sections.paragraph:
Ἰωνᾶς, ᾶ, ὁ (יוֹנָה) Jonah

① the OT prophet (4 Km 14:25; Book of Jonah; Tob 14:4, 8 BA; 3 Macc 6:8; Jos., Ant. 9, 206–14; Just.; SibOr 2, 248). He is meant by the enigmatic saying about the ‘sign of Jonah’ Mt 12:39–41; 16:4; Lk 11:29f, 32 (Mt 12:40 and AcPlCor 2:29f the sign consists in Jonah’s stay inside the monster for three days and nights. Lk thinks perh. of the preaching of the prophet to the Ninevites; s. on σημεῖον 1 and AVögtle, Synoptische Studien [Wikenhauser Festschr.] ’53, 230–77); 1 Cl 7:7.

② a Galilean fisher, father of Simon Peter and Andrew Mt 16:17 v.l. βὰρ Ἰωνᾶ (for Βαριωνᾶ); J 1:42 v.l.; 21:15–17 v.l; GHb 254, 70 (Lat.). Acc. to J 1:42; 21:15–17 his name was Ἰωάν(ν)ης (s. Ἰωάν(ν)ης 4); the J pass. all have Ἰωνᾶ as v.l., cp. Βαριωνᾶς and s. B-D-F §53, 2; W-S. §5, 26c.—On 1 and 2 HGale, JBL 60, ’41, 255–60; OGlombitza, NTS 8, ’61/62, 359–66.—REdwards, The Sign of Jonah in the Theology of the Evangelists and Q, ’71.—TW.

OT OT = Old Testament

BA BA = Biblical Archaeologist—List 6

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

AcPlCor AcPlCor = Acts of Paul: correspondence with Corinthians (a.k.a. Third Corinthians), text according to PBodmer X—List 1

perh. perh. = perhaps

Festschr. Festschr. = Festschrift, in ref. to any honorary publication

1 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

GHb GHb = Gospel according to the Hebrews—List 1

Lat. Lat. = Latin

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

W-S. W-S. = GWiner, Grammatik des neutestamentlichen Sprachidioms, ed. PSchmiedel—List 6

JBL JBL = Journal of Biblical Literature—List 6

NTS NTS = New Testament Studies—List 6

Q Q = Qumran, cited with numbers indicating cave of origin—List 2, end

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 486
